label: Lagos / Starlink Nigeria Launch
sub: Starlink began commercial service in Nigeria in early 2022 at ~$110/month, targeting rural businesses and clinics with no prior broadband access.

label: Nairobi / Digital Literacy Hub
sub: Kenya's Silicon Savannah hosts major digital literacy programs including Google's Ajira Digital and Microsoft TEALS, training over 500,000 young Kenyans in tech skills.

label: Kigali / Smart Rwanda Masterplan
sub: Rwanda's national digital agenda — Smart Rwanda 2020 — achieved 90% 4G coverage and government-subsidized devices, making it Africa's top digital inclusion success story.

label: Addis Ababa / AU Digital Strategy HQ
sub: African Union headquarters overseeing the Digital Transformation Strategy for Africa (2020–2030), targeting 75% internet penetration by 2030.

label: Dar es Salaam / Submarine Cable Hub
sub: Tanzania serves as a key landing point for SEACOM, EASSy, and DARE1 submarine cables, connecting East Africa to global internet infrastructure with >200 Gbps capacity.

label: Accra / Google & Meta Connectivity Programs
sub: Ghana hosts Meta's Terragraph urban WiFi pilot and Google's Equiano submarine cable landing station, expanding broadband capacity for West Africa.

label: Cape Town / SA Broadband Expansion
sub: South Africa's National Broadband Policy aims to connect 80% of schools and clinics to broadband by 2030. Cape Town has South Africa's highest fiber penetration at 60%.

label: Kinshasa / DRC Connectivity Gap
sub: Despite being Africa's third-largest city, Kinshasa has internet penetration below 20%. DRC overall has <10% connectivity, with 100+ million people offline — one of the world's largest unconnected populations.

label: Mumbai / Jio 5G & Digital Access
sub: Reliance Jio's 5G True launch in October 2022 started in Mumbai; Jio's JioPhone affordability program added 100+ million low-income subscribers to mobile internet since 2017.

label: New Delhi / Digital India Policy
sub: India's Digital India program — coordinated from New Delhi — aims to connect 250,000 gram panchayats (village councils) to optical fiber under BharatNet. Phase II targets all 600,000 villages.

label: Dhaka / Digital Bangladesh Initiative
sub: Bangladesh's Digital Bangladesh 2021 program achieved 95% mobile coverage; the government's Union Information Service Centers provide digital literacy to 87,000+ rural communities.

label: Manila / Philippines National Broadband
sub: The Philippines launched its National Broadband Program in 2020, aiming to connect government offices and schools with free WiFi. The Free Internet Connectivity Act covers public spaces and schools.

label: Jakarta / Palapa Ring Fiber Network
sub: Indonesia completed its Palapa Ring fiber network in 2019 — 35 fiber rings totaling 12,000 km connecting all 514 regencies. Jakarta serves as the hub for this national backbone system.

label: Yangon / Myanmar Post-Coup Connectivity Crisis
sub: Following the February 2021 military coup, Myanmar's internet penetration dropped from 42% to under 30% as the junta imposed nationwide shutdowns and forced Telenor to withdraw. Over 20 million users lost access.

label: Washington DC / Affordable Connectivity Program
sub: FCC headquarters oversaw the Affordable Connectivity Program (2021–2024), which provided $30/month broadband subsidies to 23 million low-income US households before Congress failed to renew funding in May 2024.

label: Mexico City / CFE Telecomunicaciones
sub: Mexico's state utility CFE Telecomunicaciones launched a rural internet program providing subsidized broadband to 150,000 underserved localities, using government-owned fiber infrastructure.

label: São Paulo / Brazil Digital Inclusion Programs
sub: São Paulo state runs the 'Conecta SP' free public WiFi network with 30,000 access points. Brazil's national Computadores para Inclusão program has distributed 2+ million refurbished devices to low-income communities.

label: Lima / Peru Starlink Rural Deployment
sub: Peru's Ministry of Transport contracted Starlink to connect 200+ rural schools in the Amazonian highlands where no other broadband technology was viable. The program serves 15,000+ students.

label: Port-au-Prince / Haiti Digital Exclusion
sub: Haiti has one of the Western Hemisphere's lowest internet penetration rates at ~35%, worsened by the 2010 earthquake, 2021 earthquake, and ongoing political instability. Less than 10% of rural Haitians have internet access.

label: Brussels / EU Digital Decade 2030
sub: European Commission headquarters overseeing the EU Digital Compass 2030 strategy: 100% gigabit connectivity for EU households, 5G for all populated areas, and 80% digital literacy among EU citizens.

label: Geneva / ITU Partner2Connect HQ
sub: ITU headquarters in Geneva coordinates the Partner2Connect ($40B+) and Broadband Commission, the principal international institutions tracking and funding global connectivity.

label: Kabul / Afghanistan Connectivity Regression
sub: Following the Taliban takeover in August 2021, Afghanistan's internet penetration fell from 18% to under 10%. Women were effectively banned from online educational platforms, and telecom infrastructure investment ceased.

label: Amman / Refugee Digital Inclusion Programs
sub: Jordan hosts 750,000+ Syrian refugees. UNHCR, GSMA, and Coursera partner on connectivity and digital skills programs in Za'atari and Azraq refugee camps, reaching 100,000+ refugees with free internet and training.

label: Colombo / Sri Lanka Rural Broadband
sub: Sri Lanka's National Broadband Master Plan connects rural communities via fiber and 4G. Despite economic crisis disruptions, internet penetration grew from 34% to 52% between 2020–2024.

label: Port Moresby / Pacific Islands Connectivity Gap
sub: Papua New Guinea has internet penetration below 15%, among the world's lowest. Australia, the US, and Japan have pledged $1.5 billion in Pacific cable infrastructure to counter Chinese submarine cable projects.

label: Kent, WA / Amazon Kuiper HQ
sub: Amazon Project Kuiper's satellite development and integration facility in Kent, Washington. As of April 2026, Kuiper has deployed 231 production satellites and is preparing for mid-2026 commercial launch — Starlink's first major direct competitor in the LEO broadband market.

label: New York City / Get Online NYC Campaign
sub: NYC's Office of Technology and Innovation launched the 'Get Online NYC' digital equity campaign in April 2026, connecting ~400,000 unconnected households with free internet labs, Big Apple Connect broadband subsidies, and digital literacy programs across all five boroughs.

label: Geneva / ITU 2026–2029 Connectivity Plan
sub: ITU unveiled its four-year connectivity plan (2026–2029) in April 2026 alongside the Global Connectivity Report 2025 showing 6 billion (75%) now online. The plan targets the remaining 2.2 billion unconnected through satellite expansion, rural fiber, spectrum reform, and digital skills.
